steps.title: Calculate the cost per mile driven
steps.content: Gasoline costs \$4 per gallon, and the car travels 25 miles per gallon. The cost per mile driven is $\frac{4}{25}$ dollars per mile. If Alan drives $m$ fewer miles, his total savings is $\left(\frac{4}{25}\right)m$ dollars. This tells us the coefficient must be $\frac{4}{25}$, not $\frac{25}{4}$, so we can eliminate choices A and B immediately.

steps.title: Set savings equal to the target amount
steps.content: Alan wants to reduce his weekly gas spending by \$5. His savings from driving $m$ fewer miles is $\left(\frac{4}{25}\right)m$, so the equation is $$\left(\frac{4}{25}\right)m = 5.$$ The right side must be 5 (the savings target), not 95, so choice C is eliminated. That matches choice D — D is correct.
